explanations.a: Partial. Decomposition produces both carbon and hydrogen.
explanations.c: Correct. Thermal decomposition of transformer oil releases carbon and hydrogen (and some hydrocarbons).

questions.question: Dielectric strengths of Air at NTP is

explanations.d: Correct. Air at NTP has dielectric strength of approximately 3 kV/mm (or 30 kV/cm).

questions.question: The Break Down Voltage (BDV) of transformer oil is measured when-

explanations.a: Correct. BDV test measures the voltage at which the first flashover or spark occurs.
explanations.b: Incorrect. The test stops at first flash.

questions.question: The most commonly used unit of dielectric strength is -

explanations.a: Correct. kV/mm is the most common unit for dielectric strength in practical applications.
explanations.b: V/cm is too small and less common.
explanations.c: MV/mm is too large for most materials.
explanations.d: kV/m is used for very large gaps but less common for solids/liquids.

questions.question: The maximum pour point temperature of dielectric oil is-

explanations.a: Correct. The maximum pour point for transformer oil is typically around -6°C to -9°C (IS 335 standard).
explanations.b: Too high; oil would solidify in cold weather.
